mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during easing periods, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate a growing labor force and expanding economic activity, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS levels and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 320.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 320.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance (50%) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a supply-constrained regime signals bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during supply-constrained periods are followed by positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the regime classification fails to improve predictive power over baseline.
